teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sw/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then w. Help them blend through: s-w-i-m → swim. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-w-i-m → Blend the opening cluster: sw-i-m → swim
s-w-a-m → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/a/-/m/. Blend: swam
s-w-a-n → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/a/-/n/. Blend: swan
s-w-a-t → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/a/-/t/. Blend: swat

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Were' doesn't follow normal rules. We say 'wur'. Learn it by sight.

story: The twins were at the pond for a swim. A swan swam past and they had to swat a bug!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sw/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then w. Help them blend through: s-w-i-ng → swing. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-w-i-ng → Blend the opening cluster: sw-i-ng → swing
s-w-i-f-t → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: sw-i-ft → swift
s-w-i-g → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/i/-/g/. Blend: swig
s-w-i-m → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/i/-/m/. Blend: swim

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Said' looks like s-ai-d but we say 'sed'. Learn it by sight.

story: She said the swing was swift! She had a swig of water and then a swim in the pool.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sw/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then w. Help them blend through: s-w-e-p-t → swept. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-w-e-p-t → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: sw-e-pt → swept
s-w-ee-t → The 'ee' makes one long sound. Blend: sw-ee-t → sweet
s-w-a-n → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/a/-/n/. Blend: swan
s-w-a-m → Say each sound: /s/-/w/-/a/-/m/. Blend: swam

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'sum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mum swept up some sweet crumbs. The swan swam to the edge of the pond to get some too!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /tw/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: t then w. Help them blend through: t-w-i-g → twig. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: t-w-i-g → Blend the opening cluster: tw-i-g → twig
t-w-i-n → Say each sound: /t/-/w/-/i/-/n/. Blend: twin
t-w-i-t → Say each sound: /t/-/w/-/i/-/t/. Blend: twit
t-w-i-s-t → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: tw-i-st → twist

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'a' in 'have' says /a/ not /ai/. The 'e' is silent. Learn it by sight.

story: The twin picked up a twig and gave it a twist. What a twit — he did not have a clue what to do with it!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /tw/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: t then w. Help them blend through: t-w-ee-d → tweed. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: t-w-ee-d → The 'ee' makes one long sound. Blend: tw-ee-d → tweed
t-w-i-n → Say each sound: /t/-/w/-/i/-/n/. Blend: twin
t-w-i-g → Say each sound: /t/-/w/-/i/-/g/. Blend: twig
t-w-i-s-t → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: tw-i-st → twist

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'i' says its name /ie/ and the 'e' is silent. We say 'like'. Learn it by sight.

story: The twin had a tweed hat she did like. She put a twig in it and gave it a twist for fun!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sk/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then k. Help them blend through: s-k-i-p → skip. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-k-i-p → Blend the opening cluster: sk-i-p → skip
s-k-i-d → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/d/. Blend: skid
s-k-i-n → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/n/. Blend: skin
s-k-i-ll → The 'll' is one sound. Blend: sk-i-ll → skill

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says its name /oh/. We say 'soh'. Learn it by sight.

story: She had so much skill! She could skip and not skid. She did not even graze her skin.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sk/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then k. Help them blend through: s-k-u-ll → skull. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-k-u-ll → The 'll' is one sound. Blend: sk-u-ll → skull
s-k-u-n-k → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: sk-u-nk → skunk
s-k-i-t → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/t/. Blend: skit
s-k-i-m → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/m/. Blend: skim

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Do' doesn't sound like d-o. We say 'doo'. Learn it by sight.

story: What do you see? A skull and a skunk! The kids did a skit about it and had to skim past the smelly skunk.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sk/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then k. Help them blend through: s-k-i-d → skid. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-k-i-d → Blend the opening cluster: sk-i-d → skid
s-k-i-p → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/p/. Blend: skip
s-k-u-ll → The 'll' is one sound. Blend: sk-u-ll → skull
s-k-i-n → Say each sound: /s/-/k/-/i/-/n/. Blend: skin

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'cum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Come and skip with me! Mind you do not skid on the mud or graze your skin. Look — there is a skull on that flag!

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sc/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then c. Help them blend through: s-c-a-b → scab. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-c-a-b → Blend the opening cluster: sc-a-b → scab
s-c-a-n → Say each sound: /s/-/c/-/a/-/n/. Blend: scan
s-c-a-t → Say each sound: /s/-/c/-/a/-/t/. Blend: scat
s-c-a-m → Say each sound: /s/-/c/-/a/-/m/. Blend: scam

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'There' tells us a place. The 'e-r-e' makes an 'air' sound. Learn it by sight.

story: There was a cat with a scab on its leg. It had a scan at the vet. Scat, cat! It was no scam — the cat got well.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /sc/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: s then c. Help them blend through: s-c-u-ff → scuff. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-c-u-ff → The 'ff' is one sound. Blend: sc-u-ff → scuff
s-c-o-l-d → Five sounds! Blend the opening cluster: sc-o-ld → scold
s-c-a-b → Say each sound: /s/-/c/-/a/-/b/. Blend: scab
s-c-a-n → Say each sound: /s/-/c/-/a/-/n/. Blend: scan

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'What' has a silent 'h' and the 'a' says /o/. We say 'wot'. Learn it by sight.

story: What a scuff on those new red boots! Mum will scold you. Quick, scan for a scab of polish to fix them up.
